10-science-ch1.label: Ch 1: Chemical Reactions and Equations
10-science-ch1.title: Class 10 Science: Ch 1 - Chemical Reactions and Equations

questions.q: What occurs dynamically when dilute Hydrochloric Acid is combined directly with iron filings?
options: Hydrogen gas and iron chloride are produced.
Chlorine gas and iron hydroxide are produced.
No displacement reaction takes place.
Iron salt and water form smoothly.
questions.explain: Iron reacts with dilute HCl to displace hydrogen, forming iron(II) chloride and Hydrogen gas.

questions.q: What happens when lead nitrate powder is heated strongly in a dry test tube?
options: A colorless gas with a pungent smell is evolved.
Brown fumes of nitrogen dioxide gas are evolved.
The crystals turn into a bright blue solution.
A white precipitate of lead chloride is formed.
questions.explain: Thermal decomposition of lead nitrate produces lead oxide, oxygen gas, and brown NO2 fumes.

questions.q: Which of the following processes represents a classic endothermic reaction?
options: Decomposition of vegetable matter into compost.
Combustion of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG).
Dilution of concentrated sulfuric acid with water.
Decomposition of calcium carbonate to form quicklime.
questions.explain: Breakdown of calcium carbonate requires heat absorption, making it endothermic.

questions.q: Fatty or oily food items are often flushed with nitrogen gas before packaging. What is the primary purpose?
options: To act as a natural coloring agent.
To prevent oxidation and stop the food from turning rancid.
To catalyze fermentation.
To kill viruses using nitrogen's acidic properties.
questions.explain: Nitrogen creates an oxygen-free environment, preventing oxidation that causes rancidity.

questions.q: When a shiny brown element 'X' is heated in air, it turns completely black. Identify element 'X' and the black compound formed.
options: Iron; black compound is Fe3O4.
Copper; black compound is CuO.
Aluminum; black compound is Al2O3.
Magnesium; black compound is MgO.
questions.explain: Copper reacts with oxygen when heated to form black copper(II) oxide (CuO).
